title: Dashboard E2 — fab doctor diagnostic tab integration
description: Integrate existing or planned 'fab doctor' diagnostic output as a fourth Dashboard tab view. Add GET /api/doctor endpoint in the server that runs the fab doctor health checks and returns structured diagnostic results. Add a DoctorTab view in the Preact SPA showing the diagnostic summary, check results (pass/warn/fail), and actionable fix suggestions. Depends on TASK-009 (SPA scaffold) being complete.

files.change: New Express router: GET /api/doctor runs health checks (verify .fabric/ exists, verify AGENTS.md valid, verify hooks installed, verify pre-commit hooks configured); returns {checks:[{name, status:'pass'|'warn'|'fail', message, fix?}], overall:'healthy'|'degraded'|'broken'}; reuses any existing fab doctor logic if implemented, otherwise standalone check functions

New Preact view: fetches GET /api/doctor; renders summary badge (healthy/degraded/broken); renders check list with pass(green)/warn(amber)/fail(red) status icons; expandable fix suggestion per failed check; refresh button
Add 4th nav tab '#/doctor' → DoctorTab view; update hash router
Import doctorRouter from api/doctor.ts; app.use('/api', doctorRouter)

implementation: Implement api/doctor.ts: define 4-6 health check functions (checkFabricDirExists, checkAgentsMdValid, checkHooksInstalled, checkPreCommitConfigured); each returns {name, status, message, fix?}; run all checks in parallel with Promise.all
Implement DoctorTab view with status icons and expandable fix details
Add to app navigation and api/client.ts

reference.pattern: Health check endpoint pattern + new dashboard view following TASK-009 SPA structure
reference.examples: Follow the existing view pattern from rules-tree.tsx; health check structure from any existing fab doctor implementation in packages/cli

criteria: Dashboard shows 4th tab 'Doctor' navigating to the diagnostic view
DoctorTab renders check list with correct pass/warn/fail status for a properly initialized project
DoctorTab shows fail status + fix suggestion when .fabric/ directory is missing

rationale.chosen_approach: Standalone health checks in api/doctor.ts allows integration even if full fab doctor CLI command is not yet implemented; follows v1.1 roadmap ordering (doctor + Dashboard as v1.1 Feature #5)
alternatives_considered: Wait for full fab doctor implementation (defers this E2 extension indefinitely)
Embed doctor logic in CLI only (no Dashboard visibility)
decision_factors: Dashboard + fab doctor are complementary in v1.1 roadmap
Standalone check functions can be extracted to shared fab doctor command later
rationale.tradeoffs: May duplicate some logic that a full fab doctor would eventually have; acceptable as incremental approach

manual_checks: GET http://127.0.0.1:7373/api/doctor on initialized project — verify overall:'healthy'
Remove .fabric/ and re-request — verify overall:'broken' + checkFabricDirExists status:'fail'
Navigate to Doctor tab in SPA — verify visual rendering
success_metrics: Fix suggestions appear for failed checks

risks.description: fab doctor CLI command not yet implemented, reducing data richness
risks.mitigation: Implement standalone minimal health checks in api/doctor.ts; these are useful independent of the full fab doctor command
risks.fallback: If no fab doctor data, show minimal checks for .fabric/ structure and hook installation
